steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between "photograph" and the title 'The Freeloaders'. The title identifies which specific photograph is being discussed, so the question is what punctuation (if any) belongs between the noun and its restrictive identifier.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘photograph,’: a comma before the title would make it a non-restrictive appositive, implying the title is just extra information about any photograph by Bravo. But the title is needed to specify which photograph — it is restrictive. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘photograph’: no punctuation lets 'The Freeloaders' attach directly as a restrictive identifier, which is correct when the title specifies which one. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘photograph;’: a semicolon joins two independent clauses. 'The Freeloaders' is not a clause; this creates a fragment. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘photograph:’: a colon requires a complete independent clause before it. "Lola Álvarez Bravo's photograph" is only a noun phrase, not an independent clause. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
